raw_consumer_statement:
Company : American Express Product : Credit Card Issue : Billing dispute / Failure to properly investigate Amount in dispute : {$38000.00} I am filing a complaint regarding American Expresss failure to properly investigate and resolve a billing dispute, in violation of their obligations under the Fair Credit Billing Act. 

On XX/XX/year>, I was charged XXXX amounts related to a custom engine build : {$23000.00} {$14000.00} Total : {$38000.00} The merchant ( XXXX XXXX ) accepted payment and issued a receipt for the engine build. However, no engine, parts, or services were ever delivered, and the merchant retained the full amount. 

When I disputed the charges, American Express denied the dispute stating no error found, relying on merchant-provided information that is inaccurate and internally contradictory. 

The merchant claimed that no purchase was made, yet The merchant retained over {$38000.00} in funds and refused to issue a refund. 
If no purchase occurred, the funds should have been returned immediately. 
If a purchase did occur, the merchant failed to provide the contracted service. 
Either scenario renders the charge invalid. 

American Express failed to properly evaluate the contradiction in the merchants claim. Give appropriate weight to my receipt and proof of payment, Apply the correct dispute category of Services Not Rendered / Merchandise Not Received. Conduct a reasonable investigation as required under federal law. 
I have documentation proving payment and non-delivery, which directly contradicts the merchants statements. Despite this, American Express closed the dispute without resolving the core issue : I paid for a service that was never provided. 

I am requesting that American Express : Reopen and properly investigate this dispute under the correct dispute reason Disregard inaccurate merchant claims that no purchase occurred. 
Reverse the charges and issue a full refund of {$38000.00} I am requesting CFPB assistance to ensure American Express fulfills its legal obligation to conduct a fair and accurate investigation and to correct this billing error.
